title: Node timers/promises CommonJS PromiseLike domain recovery
summary: Open the previously priced CommonJS destructuring require subset of node:timers/promises as PromiseLike receiver proof only. JS/TS lowering now emits dependency-backed ImportedBinding evidence for conservative const destructuring of setTimeout/setImmediate from require("node:timers/promises") or require("timers/promises") when require is unshadowed, letting the existing imported Promise factory materializer emit Domain(PromiseLike) without PromiseSettledValue or payload recovery.

corpus.source_scan: Targeted JS/TS source scan for CommonJS destructuring requires from node:timers/promises and timers/promises, refreshed with rg on 2026-06-29.

opened_capability: JS/TS lowering recognizes fully static const object destructuring initialized by require("node:timers/promises") or require("timers/promises").
The frontend emits ImportedBinding symbol evidence for setTimeout and setImmediate locals, including aliases, only when require is unshadowed.
The CommonJS ImportedBinding proof depends on an UnshadowedGlobal(require) symbol record, so normalize can reject the proof if that dependency is ambiguous.
The existing LibraryApiContractId::JsImportedPromiseFactory materializer then emits Domain(PromiseLike) for documented setTimeout and setImmediate arities.
closed_boundaries: No PromiseSettledValue is emitted for node timers/promises producers.
setTimeout(value).then(...) still does not recover or merge the fulfilled payload without a future scheduling/rejection-aware settled-value contract.
let and var CommonJS destructuring remain closed because the local can be reassigned.
Computed keys, default values, rest patterns, nested patterns, dynamic require arguments, and shadowed require remain closed.
Namespace/default import member calls and broad require namespace/member calls remain outside this slice.
Unsupported arities remain closed for result-domain materialization.
Timer delay, ordering, AbortSignal cancellation, and broad scheduling equivalence remain unmodeled.
soundness_notes: The CommonJS path reuses the same ImportedBinding semantic coordinate as ESM imports instead of adding a selector-specific Promise shortcut.
The require proof is a dependency of the binding proof, and normalize requires all binding dependencies to be asserted before materializing PromiseLike domain evidence.
The binding evidence uses the variable declarator span so imported occurrence validation can compose with the existing destructuring alias assignment span.
source_scan_commands: for f in bench/repos/jest/e2e/circus-concurrent/__tests__/concurrent-mixed.test.js bench/repos/jest/e2e/circus-concurrent/__tests__/concurrent.test.js bench/repos/jest/e2e/circus-concurrent/__tests__/concurrent-skip.test.js bench/repos/jest/e2e/circus-concurrent/__tests__/concurrent-only.test.js bench/repos/jest/packages/jest-circus/src/__tests__/concurrent.test.ts; do rg -o "setTimeout\\(" "$f"; done | wc -l
